narrative.headline: 2018 Honda Ridgeline: electrical issues lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 157 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 31%, followed by back-over prevention (19%) and the engine (13%). The reports are mild by outcome: no fires, no injuries, and no deaths anywhere, with just a single crash tied to the electrical system. Many complaints center on electronics and fueling rather than safety failures.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2018 HONDA RIDGELINE. THE CONTACT STATED THAT THE SAFETY SYSTEM FEATURE THAT DETECTS SURROUNDING VEHICLES DID NOT FUNCTION. IN ADDITION, THE CONTACT STATED THAT THE BRAKES DID NOT ACTIVATE IN ORDER TO AVOID A CRASH WHEN THE VEHICLE WAS TOO CLOSE TO THE VEHI
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 49 reports (31%), with 1 crash and no fires or injuries. Worth checking the electronics on a test drive.

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 30 reports (19%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several owners describe backup-camera failures.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 21 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Listen for any knocking noises from the engine.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 17 reports (11%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Complaints often involve the fuel filler area.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: The fewest reports here: 13 (8%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several describe trouble at the fuel filler when refueling.
